summary: Amaranth tolerates heat and moderate water stress once established; still needs establishment water.

steps.title: Establishment
steps.body: Water seedlings regularly for 2–3 weeks; reduce frequency as roots deepen.

hazard_links.mitigation_detail: Row cover during peak flea-beetle pressure; rotate with non-host crops; hand-pick or use labeled controls per extension.

climate_context: Example (India / global): tropical to subtropical; often grown kharif with monsoon rain. USDA zones optional; use local ag advisories for your state.

summary: Grow in imported clean soil in raised beds to bypass unknown native urban soil.

steps.title: Bed fill
steps.body: Use tested compost and topsoil from reputable suppliers; capillary break if needed.

hazard_links.mitigation_detail: Raised bed with clean fill reduces uptake pathway from legacy site soil.
hazard_links.evidence_notes: Still test irrigation water if suspect.

summary: Epiphytic orchid that grows in indirect light with high humidity. Requires well-draining bark medium and careful watering to avoid root rot.

steps.title: Potting
steps.body: Use orchid bark mix in a well-draining pot with airflow around roots.

steps.title: Placement and light
steps.body: Place the orchid in bright, indirect light. Avoid direct sunlight as it can burn the leaves.

steps.title: Watering
steps.body: Water when roots turn silvery or dry. Soak thoroughly and allow complete drainage. Do not let water sit in the pot.

steps.title: Humidity control
steps.body: Maintain humidity around 50–70 percent using misting or a humidity tray. Ensure airflow to prevent fungal issues.

steps.title: Feeding
steps.body: Apply diluted orchid fertilizer every 2–4 weeks during active growth.

steps.title: Flowering and care
steps.body: Encourage blooming by slightly lowering night temperature. Remove dead flowers and trim spikes after blooming.

hazard_links.mitigation_detail: Avoid water accumulation in roots, ensure airflow, and inspect regularly for pests like mites or aphids.

climate_context: Tropical, humid environments; thrives indoors in warm climates. Köppen: Af, Am.

summary: A dyed variant of Phalaenopsis orchid. Requires indirect light, high humidity, and a well-draining bark medium. Blue coloration fades after blooming.

steps.title: Potting
steps.body: Use orchid bark mix in a well-draining container with airflow around roots.

steps.title: Light
steps.body: Place in bright, indirect light. Avoid direct sunlight.

steps.title: Watering
steps.body: Water when roots turn silvery. Allow full drainage and avoid water buildup.

steps.title: Humidity
steps.body: Maintain 50–70 percent humidity with airflow.

steps.title: Feeding
steps.body: Use diluted orchid fertilizer every 2–4 weeks.

steps.title: Flower cycle
steps.body: Expect blue flowers only once; future blooms return to natural color (usually white).

hazard_links.mitigation_detail: Avoid excess water, ensure airflow, and monitor for pests regularly.

climate_context: Tropical, humid indoor conditions. Thrives in warm environments with stable humidity.

summary: Cool-season crop grown for underground tubers. Requires loose, well-drained soil and consistent moisture. Sensitive to heat and frost.

steps.title: Planting
steps.body: Plant seed potatoes 5–10 cm deep in loose soil with eyes facing upward.

steps.title: Sprouting
steps.body: Allow shoots to emerge; ensure full sunlight and moderate watering.

steps.title: Growth
steps.body: Maintain soil moisture and remove weeds. Avoid waterlogging.

steps.title: Hilling
steps.body: Pile soil around the base of plants to cover developing tubers and prevent greening.

steps.title: Maturation
steps.body: Reduce watering as plants mature. Leaves will begin to yellow.

steps.title: Harvest
steps.body: Harvest when foliage dies back. Dig carefully to avoid damaging tubers.

requirements.notes: Harvest time depends on variety and growing conditions.
Full sun required for optimal tuber development.
Space plants adequately to allow tuber expansion.
Cool-season crop; high temperatures reduce yield.
Moderate watering; avoid waterlogging. Increase during tuber formation.

hazard_links.mitigation_detail: Keep tubers covered to prevent greening. Avoid overwatering and ensure airflow. Monitor for pests and remove affected plants early.

climate_context: Temperate climates; grows best in 15–25°C. Common in highland and cooler regions. Köppen: Cfb, Cwb.
